parameters.name: tr
parameters.override_behavior: sets rise and fall smoothing for out and metric without changing segment selection
parameters.valid_range: finite real; use tr >= 0 for physical transition smoothing

evaluation_summary.full_credit: valid candidate and reference pass and all five negatives killed behaviorally

modeling_constraints: Use deterministic voltage-coded ramp, chirp, and gated burst generation.
Use smoothed voltage contributions only; absolute transient time may shape public stimulus segments.
Do not use current contributions, ddt(), idt(), transistor-level devices, waveform tables, validation hooks, or simulator-specific side channels.

properties.observable_contract: When rst is above the 0.45 V control threshold, out is held near 0.45 V and metric is low.
For mode below 0.30 V outside reset, out produces a monotonic ramp segment from about 0.18 V toward 0.45 V and metric is near 0.20 V.
For mode from 0.30 V through below 0.60 V, out is a sine segment centered near 0.45 V whose instantaneous frequency increases over the segment, with metric near 0.50 V.
For mode at or above 0.60 V and gate high, out produces a deterministic PRBS-like burst between the low and high stimulus levels.
In burst mode with gate low, out returns near 0.45 V and metric reports the idle rather than active-burst status.
Mode and gate behavior follows the voltage-coded inputs over arbitrary legal control schedules rather than a fixed stimulus timeline.

title: Programmable Stimulus Sequencer
